title: sync-reenable-merge-cross-check: drop the touched_by_merge guard
body:
# Goal

Remove the `if !op_view.touched_by_merge` guard at
`crates/jjf-storage/src/read.rs:85` so the `cross_check`
between the file's serialized record and the op-replay
projection runs for merged op-views too.

Today the cross-check is skipped after any merge because
the file-bytes merge driver (legacy, from `e2e473b`) could
produce a file that disagreed with op-replay's last-write.
With op-space resolution (`bfc732b`) the file IS a
deterministic projection of the op chain, so the
disagreement window should be closed.

End state: cross-check runs unconditionally; any divergence
it surfaces is either a real bug (fix), a known limitation
of the resolver (document with a focused skip + test), or
a stale invariant in `cross_check` itself (update).

# Why now

The guard documents itself as "skip; revisit after
op-space lands." Op-space landed. The cross-check is a
free debug-build safety net that catches resolver bugs
the moment they happen rather than letting them propagate
into the read path silently. Leaving it disabled is
strictly worse than re-enabling and triaging whatever it
finds.

# Why blocked by `sync-resolve-divergence-tests`

If the cross-check surfaces real divergences, the
resolver-test suite from `1c20aa3` gives us a place to
write minimal repros without having to scaffold a full
sync test repo. Land the tests first; this ticket builds
on that foundation.

# Scope

## Code change

- Remove the `if !op_view.touched_by_merge` guard at
  `read.rs:85`. `cross_check(&record, &comments,
  &op_view)` runs unconditionally (still only in debug
  builds, per its existing `debug_assert`-shaped
  invariants).
- Likely also remove the `touched_by_merge: bool` field
  on `OpView` (`read.rs:202`) and its setters
  (`read.rs:289`, `read.rs:329`) — once the guard is
  gone, the flag has no consumer. Confirm with `cargo
  check` and remove the dead code.

## Test path

- `cargo nextest run --workspace` is the primary signal.
- Any test that exercises a merge AND triggers the
  cross-check is the load-bearing one. Existing sync
  integration tests should produce merged op-views.
- If a real divergence surfaces:
  - Write a focused unit test against `resolve_divergence`
    that reproduces it (uses the `1c20aa3` test
    scaffolding).
  - Fix the resolver if the divergence is a real bug.
  - If the divergence is the comment at `read.rs:78-84`
    foresaw — file content reflects merge-driver winner
    while op-replay reflects last-applied — either:
    - Enrich the merge trailer with per-field
      `Jjf-Resolved-*` payload so op-replay can be
      authoritative across merges (the comment's
      forward-looking plan), or
    - Adjust `cross_check` to tolerate the specific
      class of divergence with a documented reason.

# Acceptance

- [ ] `if !op_view.touched_by_merge` guard removed from
  `read.rs`.
- [ ] `touched_by_merge` field removed from `OpView` if
  no remaining consumer.
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` green.
- [ ] If any divergence surfaced: a regression test
  pinning the resolved behavior, plus a closing-comment
  note describing what was found.
- [ ] `read.rs` comment block at lines 78-84 updated to
  reflect the new state (or removed entirely if the
  forward-looking caveat no longer applies).

# Out of scope

- Adding new merge-policy features.
- Reshaping `OpView` beyond removing the now-dead flag.
- Performance work on the cross-check (it's debug-only).
- Rewriting `cross_check` itself unless a divergence
  forces it.

# Pointers

- Guard site: `crates/jjf-storage/src/read.rs:85`.
- Flag declaration: `read.rs:202`.
- Flag setters: `read.rs:289`, `read.rs:329`.
- `cross_check` definition: `read.rs:343`.
- The forward-looking comment: `read.rs:78-84`.
- Resolver: `crates/jjf-storage/src/merge_ops.rs`.
- Spec: `docs/storage-format.md` §6 (per-field merge policy).
